accept: A canonical pre-close check exists (Makefile target, .act/hooks/ script, or both) that runs gofmt -l, go vet, go test ./...
Skill or CLAUDE.md instructs agents to run the check before act close (not just before push).
If a hook is used, it integrates with the existing internal/hooks/ infrastructure and respects per_session bundling (doesn't fight the auto-commit flow).
Tested: an agent simulating the act-728d failure (uncommitted gofmt drift) is caught by the check before close.
payload.description:
Discovered 2026-05-10: the act-728d bundling agent shipped without running gofmt on their new files; CI failed on every push for a dozen pushes until manually noticed and fixed. The agent's self-review caught a real bug (opsDir path) but didn't run the actual lint/format/test gates the CI cares about.

The act repo's CI runs 'gofmt -l . | grep .' as a fail gate, plus 'go vet ./...' and 'go test ./...'. Agents working in worktrees don't reliably run all three before close. Two-part fix:

1. **Pre-close hook OR Makefile target** that runs gofmt -l, go vet, go test ./... and refuses to proceed if any fails. Could be a 'make pre-close' target the canonical loop documents, or a hook in .act/hooks/ (the directory exists and is wired through internal/hooks/).

2. **CLAUDE.md / skill update** to require running this check before 'act close', not just before push. Lessons-from-this-incident in versioning rationale.

Bonus: 'act doctor' could grow a check that the latest commit on the current branch passes gofmt — making CI failures locally-detectable before push.
payload.title: pre-close check: gofmt + tests must pass before close commit
